content: Just as the original Reflexion framework for autonomous agents leverages dynamic self-reflection on past actions to improve task performance over time, high school English language learners can use a structured Reflexion practice modeled after LLM self-reflection's multiple inconsistent solving perspectives to iteratively refine their creative writing output. This practice would ask learners to contrast their first draft of a narrative or argument against two alternative conflicting perspectives on their core message, just as LLM self-reflection methods like Self-Contrast use inconsistent problem-solving approaches to expose flawed reasoning.
